pattern: The median is a partition point: binary-search the smaller array for the split that balances the two halves, giving a logarithmic merge.
hints: You never need to fully merge the arrays - you only need the one or two values that land in the middle.
Imagine cutting both arrays so the combined left half has exactly the right number of elements; the median lives at that boundary.

Binary-search a cut position in the shorter array (the other cut is forced by the total length). A cut is valid when every left value is <= every right value across both arrays; then the median comes from the boundary values, taking a float average when the total length is even.

edge_inputs.description: one array empty, even total averages the middle pair
disjoint ranges where all of one array precedes the other

stress.description: two large sorted arrays of random integers; median compared with tolerance
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = max(1, size)
    m = rng.randrange(1, n + 1)
    a = sorted(rng.randrange(-1000000, 1000001) for _ in range(m))
    b = sorted(rng.randrange(-1000000, 1000001) for _ in range(n))
    return [a, b]
stress.note: at least one element total; both arrays kept sorted by construction
